dimensions.summary: Issue 129 defines the semantic owner, inputs, provenance model, unusual-scenario confirmation, closed loop, four exits, script boundary, distribution scope, exclusions, and observable acceptance criteria.
The request is one Phase 1 delivery unit: migrate planning approval semantics into guru-approve-task-plan while retaining one existing artifact and thin workflow routing.

Current code identifies the canonical Guru Skill package roots, Phase 1 workflow block, shared planning recorder/checker, preset registries, platform discovery copies, tests, and install verification targets.
The current workflow still expands planning review and confirmation semantics, while the shared runtime owns only the existing artifact projection and deterministic checks.
No guru-approve-task-plan package or stable typed-exit owner exists, so the requested semantic-owner migration remains unimplemented.
Durable docs, current workflow/runtime, and tests agree on the existing planning gate and expose the same missing semantic Skill owner without a conflicting implementation.
The delivery reuses the planning ambiguity scanner, explicit post-planning confirmation, and ambiguity review mechanisms while rejecting historical upstream-owned overlay edits.
The current open-issue duplicate search is empty; adjacent Phase 2, Branch Review, and legacy-overlay issues are downstream units rather than replacements for issue 129.
The live open issue title, body, URL, update time, target disposition, and wording evidence all bind the same current authority.
Current context, clarity, wording, target, base, history, duplicate, and disposition identities form one complete checked linkage.

scope_conclusion.requirement_scope_basis: The live issue 129 body, current clarification result, passed wording review, official Trellis extension contracts, current docs/code/tests, and selected archived planning mechanisms define the delivery scope.
scope_conclusion.duplicate_reuse_decision: No open duplicate replaces issue 129; issues 130, 131, and 132 remain separate downstream delivery units.
scope_conclusion.implementation_target: Canonical guru-approve-task-plan package and interface, shared planning-approval recorder/checker evolution, thin Phase 1 workflow route, additive preset/platform discovery, durable docs/spec, and dogfood/throwaway/update tests.
scope_conclusion.current_gap: Planning approval semantics are still expanded in workflow text and no standalone/workflow closed-loop Skill owns adequacy, provenance, dedicated scope confirmation, revision, or four typed exits.
archived_constraints: Reuse the issue 93 planning ambiguity scanner and fixed planning scope.
Preserve issue 52 explicit post-planning confirmation as distinct from generic continuation.
Reuse issue 83 planning ambiguity review without restoring upstream-owned overlays.
risk_boundary: Normal honest workflow operation, ordinary stale or mismatched evidence, common user mistakes, and correctness or compatibility boundaries only.
Hashes and freshness bind versions and consistency; they are not hostile-input security boundaries.
excluded_scope: Changes to upstream Trellis skills or agents.
Malicious or adversarial artifact forgery and new concurrency, TOCTOU, fault-injection, crash-consistency, or cross-OS hardening.

ai_review_gate.summary: All ten readiness dimensions pass against current linked evidence. Issue 129 is one independently deliverable and independently verifiable Phase 1 Skill migration.

human_confirmation.reason: The review preserves confirmed product semantics and introduces no new scope proposal.

reason: Issue 129 is current, bounded, non-duplicate, implementation-incomplete, and ready for workspace creation.

affected_evidence.summary: The checked portable linkage across target, context, clarity, wording, base, history, and duplicate evidence.
